initial_program:
% leftmost nonzero cell in a row
leftmost(R, L, Color) :- input(R, L, Color), Color != 0,
    #count{ X : input(R, X, Z0), Z0 != 0, X < L } = #count{ X : input(R, X, 0), X < L }.

% rows that contain no nonzero colour (i.e. all zeros)
row_all_zero(R) :- #count{ X : input(R, X, Z0), Z0 != 0 } = 0.

% there are at least two zeroes left of the leftmost cell
manyZeros(R, L) :- #count{ X : input(R, X, 0), X < L } > 1.

% zero cells left of the leftmost cell  colour the zeros
output(R, C, Color) :- leftmost(R, L, Color), C < L, input(R, C, 0), manyZeros(R, L).

% zero cells to the right of the leftmost cell when manyZeros is true (still zero)
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, L, Color), C >= L, manyZeros(R, L).

% zero cells left of the leftmost cell when manyZeros is false (keep zero)
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, L, Color), C < L, not manyZeros(R, L).

% zero cells to the right of the leftmost cell when manyZeros is false (keep zero)
output(R, C, 0) :- leftmost(R, L, Color), C >= L, not manyZeros(R, L).

% rows where every cell is zero  copy the zeros unchanged
output(R, C, 0) :- row_all_zero(R), input(R, C, 0).

% copy all nonzero cells
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

#show output/3.
